zh.lifeSummary: 當Hugh de Beauchamp於1290年出生在英國薩默塞特利勒達時，他的父親Humphrey de Beauchamp11287歲，母親Sybil Oliver36歲。他於大約1314年與Idonea Lisle在英國薩默塞特結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1337年在他的家鄉享年47歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Hugh de Beauchamp于1290年出生在英国薩默塞特利勒達时，他的父亲Humphrey de Beauchamp11287岁，母亲Sybil Oliver36岁。他于大约1314年与Idonea Lisle在英国薩默塞特结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1337年在他的家乡享年47岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Beachum
meanings: Altered form of English   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: English:
possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Hugh de Beauchamp was born in 1290, in Lillesdon, Somerset, England, his father, Humphrey de Beauchamp, was 11287 and his mother, Sybil Oliver, was 36. He married Idonea Lisle about 1314, in Somerset, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died in 1337, in his hometown, at the age of 47.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hugh de Beauchamp est né en 1290, à Lillesdon, Somerset, Angleterre, son père, Humphrey de Beauchamp, avait 11287 ans et sa mère, Sybil Oliver, avait 36 ans. Il a épousé Idonea Lisle environ 1314, à Somerset, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1337, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 47 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Hugh de Beauchamp 1290 in Lillesdon, Somerset, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Humphrey de Beauchamp 11287 und seine Mutter Sybil Oliver 36. Er hat um 1314 in Somerset, England Idonea Lisle geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1337 im Alter von 47 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Hugh de Beauchamp è nato il nel 1290 nel Lillesdon, Somerset, England, il suo padre, Humphrey de Beauchamp, aveva 11287 anni e il suo madre, Sybil Oliver, aveva 36 anni. Lui sposato Idonea Lisle circa 1314, nel Somerset, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1337, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 47 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Hugh de Beauchampが1290年にイギリスソメルセトリレスドンで生まれた時，父Humphrey de Beauchampは11287歳，母Sybil Oliverは36歳であった。約1314年にイギリスソメルセトでIdonea Lisleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1337年に故郷で47歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Hugh de Beauchamp(이)가 1290년 영국 서머세트 릴레즈던에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Humphrey de Beauchamp(은)는 11287세였고, 그의 어머니 Sybil Oliver(은)는 36세였다. 그는 대략 1314년 영국 서머세트에서 Idonea Lisle(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1337년 그의 고향에서 47세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Hugh de Beauchamp nasceu em 1290, em Lillesdon, Somerset, Inglaterra, seu pai, Humphrey de Beauchamp, tinha 11287 anos e sua mãe, Sybil Oliver, tinha 36 anos. Ele casou-se com Idonea Lisle aproximadamente 1314, em Somerset, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1337, em sua cidade natal, com 47 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hugh de Beauchamp родился в 1290 г. в местности лиллесдон, сомирсет, Англия, его отцу по имени Humphrey de Beauchamp было 11287 лет, а матери, которую звали Sybil Oliver, 36. Он женился на женщине по имени Idonea Lisle около 1314 г. в местности сомирсет, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1337 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 47 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Hugh de Beauchamp nació en 1290, en Lillesdon, Somerset, Inglaterra, su papá, Humphrey de Beauchamp, tenía 11287 años y su mamá, Sybil Oliver, tenía 36 años. Se casó con Idonea Lisle aproximadamente 1314, en Somerset, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió en 1337, en su pueblo, a la edad de 47 años.
